title: Chamaerhodiola algida
intro: Chamaerhodiola algida, also known as the Siberian rhodiola or Arctic rhodiola, is a type of flowering plant in the family Crassulaceae that is native to the Arctic regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used to enhance mental and physical performance, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve overall mood due to its adaptogenic properties. The bioactive constituents of Chamaerhodiola algida include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Chamaerhodiola algida is commonly used to make herbal supplements, teas, and extracts, which are often standardized to contain high levels of its active compounds, rosin and rhodioloside.
taxonomy: Chamaerhodiola algida is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. It is a perennial plant that grows in alpine and subalpine regions. Chamaerhodiola algida is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. Within the Brassicaceae family, Chamaerhodiola algida is placed in the genus Chamaerhodiola.
